name: Koide formula for charged-lepton masses
summary: The empirical relation Q = (m_e + m_mu + m_tau) / (sqrt(m_e) + sqrt(m_mu) + sqrt(m_tau))^2 = 2/3, discovered by Yoshio Koide in 1981-1983, holds to 5 significant digits using pole-mass values of the charged leptons. The relation has no derivation from any standard-model or beyond-SM framework, yet stubbornly persists as more precise lepton-mass measurements appear. It saturates the algebraic bound 1/3 <= Q <= 1 (Cauchy-Schwarz lower limit, equal-mass upper limit) at a non-trivial point. Either a profound clue to lepton-mass generation or one of the most striking numerical coincidences in physics.

characteristic_numbers.note: Computed using PDG 2024 pole masses: m_e = 0.51099895 MeV, m_mu = 105.6583755 MeV, m_tau = 1776.86 MeV. Agreement with 2/3 at parts per million.

principles_stressed.note: Q = 2/3 is a measure-zero point in lepton-mass space - obtaining it from any generic Yukawa structure requires precise cancellations. Proposed origins include democratic mass matrix (M_ij = m sqrt(m_i m_j) / 3) plus a circular permutation; SU(3)_L family symmetry; preon binding-energy arguments (Foot, 1994); but no derivation reproduces the precision of the empirical fit while predicting other charged-lepton observables. Quark-mass analogues fail badly (Q_quark != 2/3).

history: Koide (1981), 'A new view of quark and lepton mass hierarchy', PRD 28, 252. Koide (1983), 'A fermion-boson composite model of quarks and leptons', Phys. Lett. B 120, 161. Brannen (2006) noted geometric interpretation in eigenvalue triangle. Foot (1994), 'A note on Koide's lepton mass relation', arXiv:hep-ph/9402242. Updated-precision discussions: Sumino (2008, arXiv:0812.2103); Cao-Ma (2012, arXiv:1208.4392). Status remains: empirically robust, theoretically unexplained.

references.citation: Koide, 'A fermion-boson composite model of quarks and leptons', Phys. Lett. B 120, 161 (1983)
Foot, 'A note on Koide's lepton mass relation', arXiv:hep-ph/9402242 (1994)
